name: sql-patterns
description: Quick reference for common SQL patterns, CTEs, window functions, and indexing strategies. Triggers on: sql patterns, cte example, window functions, sql join, index strategy, pagination sql.
capabilities: CTE examples with chained common table expressions
Window function patterns (ROW_NUMBER, RANK, LAG, LEAD)
JOIN reference with inner, left, right, and full joins
Pagination patterns using OFFSET/LIMIT and keyset pagination
Index types including B-tree, hash, GIN, and GiST
Anti-patterns to avoid with fixes and explanations
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing SQL reference patterns. No executable code, no file system access beyond reading its own markdown files, no network calls, no command execution. All static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting markdown formatting and SQL function names.
valueStatement: Writing efficient SQL queries requires knowing common patterns and anti-patterns. This skill provides ready-to-use examples for CTEs, window functions, joins, and indexing strategies.
limitations: Does not execute queries or connect to databases
Pattern examples may require adjustment for specific database systems
Does not generate custom queries from user requirements

useCases.title: Query optimization

promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to write a CTE in SQL

promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use SUM() OVER for running totals

promptTemplates.title: Index strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: What index type should I use for JSONB queries

promptTemplates.title: Join types
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN

bestPractices: List columns explicitly instead of using SELECT *
Use keyset pagination (WHERE id > X) instead of OFFSET for large datasets
Place the most selective column first in composite indexes
antiPatterns: Using WHERE YEAR(date) = 2024 instead of date range predicates
Using NOT IN with NULL values causing unexpected results
Writing N+1 queries instead of using JOIN or batch operations

faq.question: Which databases support window functions?
faq.answer: Most modern databases support window functions including PostgreSQL, MySQL 8+, SQL Server, Oracle, and BigQuery.

faq.question: What is the maximum offset for pagination?
faq.answer: Avoid large OFFSET values. Keyset pagination with WHERE clause performs better for deep pagination.

faq.question: Can I use these patterns with ORMs?
faq.answer: Yes. Patterns translate to raw SQL. Some ORM query builders can express CTEs and window functions.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only provides reference documentation. It never connects to databases or processes user data.

faq.question: Why is my query slow despite having an index?
faq.answer: Check if the query uses the indexed column in functions. Also verify column order matches the leftmost prefix rule.

faq.question: How does this differ from online SQL documentation?
faq.answer: This skill provides curated, verified patterns specifically formatted for AI assistants to use in context.
